items.q: What is a tone row in serial music?
items.correct: An ordered series of all 12 chromatic pitches, used as the basis of a piece.
items.misconception: A row of notes played together as a chord.
items.random_wrong: Any sequence of notes a composer chooses.
items.why: Students miss the SPECIFIC structure. Schoenberg's 12-tone technique uses ALL 12 pitches in a fixed order before any repeats, generating melodies and harmonies from that row.

items.correct: Rapid alternation between the written note and the note a step above.
items.why: Students confuse trill (rapid alternation between two notes) with vibrato (pitch oscillation around one note). Trills add ornamentation; vibrato adds expressive width to a sustained pitch.

items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: Whose 1973 Structure of Atonal Music codified set classes?
items.why: It's Forte. “Lewin” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Whose 1998 Classical Form defines thematic functions?
items.why: It's Caplin. “Hepokoski” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Schenker publish Der freie Satz?
items.why: The real figure is 1935 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
